title: Unable to delete from local docker registry
body:
I have reviewed similar tickets in this repo but unfortunately have not been able to resolve my issue. It may be PBKC but i would like to confirm... :)

I am currently unable to delete a repo from my local registry. 

**inspect**

```
$ skopeo inspect docker://registry.local:5000/el7-base       
{
    "Name": "registry.local:5000/el7-base",
    "Digest": "sha256:2c9af36296b684757875b9a8e564861d0e4780caa4385ee64ab5a503a1e68c9f",
    "RepoTags": [
        "latest"
    ],
    "Created": "2019-06-06T02:04:12.567754296Z",
    "DockerVersion": "",
    "Labels": {
        "name": "el7-base"
    },
    "Architecture": "amd64",
    "Os": "linux",
    "Layers": [
        "sha256:677db987b43893305542e8385b60c5aae2a7f0a0ee86fd09f44e8d2082c1bce4"
    ]
}
```

**delete**

```
$ skopeo --debug delete --tls-verify=false docker://registry.local:5000/el7-base       
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration 
DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration        
DEBU[0000]   Using file:///var/lib/atomic/sigstore      
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/registry.local:5000 
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry.local:5000/v2/          
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry.local:5000/v2/ err Get https://registry.local:5000/v2/: http: server gave HTTP response to HTTPS client (&url.Error{Op:"Get", URL:"https://registry.local:5000/v2/", Err:(*errors.errorString)(0xc000450890)}) 
DEBU[0000] GET http://registry.local:5000/v2/           
DEBU[0000] Ping http://registry.local:5000/v2/ status 200 
DEBU[0000] GET http://registry.local:5000/v2/el7-base/manifests/latest 
FATA[0000] Unable to delete registry.local:5000/el7-base:latest. Image may not exist or is not stored with a v2 Schema in a v2 registry
```

**skopeo info**

```
$ skopeo --version
skopeo version 0.1.36-dev
```

* Fedora 30, installed with `dnf`

comments.body:
The registry needs to be configured to support remote delete.
@jwhonce Do you remember how to configure this?

The man page is supposed to say>

Additionally, the registry must allow deletions by setting `REGISTRY_STORAGE_DELETE_ENABLED=true` for the registry daemon.

@rhatdan Thanks for the response. This is most likely the issue as my registry was initiated with the default: `podman run -d -p 5000:5000 --restart=always --name registry registry:2` as per: https://docs.docker.com/registry/

Thanks for your report.

Yes, the registry must be configured to allow deletion, but…
> ```
> $ skopeo inspect docker://registry.local:5000/el7-base       
> ```
> <results>
> 
> ```
> $ skopeo --debug delete --tls-verify=false docker://registry.local:5000/el7-base       
> DEBU[0000] GET http://registry.local:5000/v2/el7-base/manifests/latest 
> <snip>
> FATA[0000] Unable to delete registry.local:5000/el7-base:latest. Image may not exist or is not stored with a v2 Schema in a v2 registry
> ```
This is actually reported when the image is not found at all; the code did not even try deleting it. Yet, `skopeo inspect` above did find it. Are those the actual outputs, or have they been edited?

(And just guessing - if they have not been edited, is it possible that the image uses schema1? I.e. what is the output of `skopeo inspect --raw docker://registry.local:5000/el7-base`?

Thanks for the response @mtrmac . 

> Are those the actual outputs, or have they been edited?

Yes, these are the actual outputs. 
![Screenshot from 2019-06-08 10-51-26](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/21171128/59139991-682af680-89db-11e9-9637-cfb0e416910f.png)

>what is the output of skopeo inspect --raw docker://registry.local:5000/el7-base?

```
$ skopeo inspect --raw docker://registry.local:5000/el7-base | jq .
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "config": {
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.config.v1+json",
    "digest": "sha256:b60e0c8ed5c1967c0d20c4796cd133f916d756652fcdb3df475fd25600ddb067",
    "size": 412
  },
  "layers": [
    {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar",
      "digest": "sha256:677db987b43893305542e8385b60c5aae2a7f0a0ee86fd09f44e8d2082c1bce4",
      "size": 24647582
    }
  ]
}
```

On further investigation, it appears this may be an issue in how either myself or buildah is creating the container manifests. Please see below for breakdown. 

1. starting my registry with `REGISTRY_STORAGE_DELETE_ENABLED=true` does indeed enable remote deletion. However, this appears to be only successful on images im pulling from docker.io. I am still unable to delete images that I am creating `(buildah from scratch)`

```
$ podman run golang
Trying to pull docker.io/library/golang...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob aa24759e848f done
Copying blob c5e155d5a1d1 done
Copying blob 3b7ca19181b2 done
Copying blob 4250b3117dca done
Copying blob 221d80d00ae9 done
Copying blob 927e9eaeed19 done
Copying blob 66293f4dacbd done
Copying config 7ced090ee8 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
$ podman tag docker.io/library/golang registry.local:5000/golang
$ podman push registry.local:5000/golang
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 2c719774c1e1 done
Copying blob ec62f19bb3aa done
Copying blob 4879ce009c87 done
Copying blob 4230ff7f2288 done
Copying blob f94641f1fe1f done
Copying blob 16f63bc6cca8 done
Copying blob d3c12cc41aa1 done
Copying config 7ced090ee8 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
$ ./skopeo --debug delete --tls-verify=false docker://registry.local:5000/golang
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration 
DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration        
DEBU[0000]   Using file:///var/lib/atomic/sigstore      
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/registry.local:5000 
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry.local:5000/v2/          
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry.local:5000/v2/ err Get https://registry.local:5000/v2/: http: server gave HTTP response to HTTPS client (&url.Error{Op:"Get", URL:"https://registry.local:5000/v2/", Err:(*errors.errorString)(0xc00050eb60)}) 
DEBU[0000] GET http://registry.local:5000/v2/           
DEBU[0000] Ping http://registry.local:5000/v2/ status 200 
DEBU[0000] GET http://registry.local:5000/v2/golang/manifests/latest 
DEBU[0000] DELETE http://registry.local:5000/v2/golang/manifests/sha256:b0171f45b8cc75805ab589094c8b07084f73bfae784c316868983a494d294c5f 
DEBU[0000] Deleting /var/lib/atomic/sigstore/golang@sha256=b0171f45b8cc75805ab589094c8b07084f73bfae784c316868983a494d294c5f/signature-1
```

Where as my builds...

```
$ podman push registry.local:5000/el7
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5b9daeb580c1 done
Copying config c8699e09c0 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
$ ./skopeo --debug delete --tls-verify=false docker://registry.local:5000/el7   
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration 
DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration        
DEBU[0000]   Using file:///var/lib/atomic/sigstore      
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/registry.local:5000 
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry.local:5000/v2/          
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry.local:5000/v2/ err Get https://registry.local:5000/v2/: http: server gave HTTP response to HTTPS client (&url.Error{Op:"Get", URL:"https://registry.local:5000/v2/", Err:(*errors.errorString)(0xc00044f490)}) 
DEBU[0000] GET http://registry.local:5000/v2/           
DEBU[0000] Ping http://registry.local:5000/v2/ status 200 
DEBU[0000] GET http://registry.local:5000/v2/el7/manifests/latest 
FATA[0000] Unable to delete registry.local:5000/el7:latest. Image may not exist or is not stored with a v2 Schema in a v2 registry
 Status: 404
manifestBody: {"errors":[{"code":"MANIFEST_UNKNOWN","message":"OCI manifest found, but accept header does not support OCI manifests"}]}
```

Please note, I added some extra debugging in that output:

```
return errors.Errorf("Unable to delete %v. Image may not exist or is not stored with a v2 Schema in a v2 registry\n Status: %v\nmanifestBody: %s", ref.ref, get.StatusCode, manifestBody)
```

In comparing the inspect outputs of the golang and my el7 images, it appears that I may be missing a header...

```
$ ./skopeo inspect --raw --tls-verify=false docker://registry.local:5000/el7 | jq .
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "config": {
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.config.v1+json",
    "digest": "sha256:c8699e09c0706d8576f509f2fe33d6ac7f3127338ed7bf66af85382470f61580",
    "size": 393
  },
```

```
$ ./skopeo  inspect --raw --tls-verify=false docker://registry.local:5000/golang | jq .
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
  "config": {
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.container.image.v1+json",
    "size": 5464,
    "digest": "sha256:7ced090ee82ee77beabd76ad1ba3b167acd8609b0b10c4ef46cee3ddf6e6fa5f"
  },
```

Notice the lack of ` "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",` in my el7 image?

```
/ # registry -v
registry github.com/docker/distribution v2.7.1
```

This does not comply with the current code block of skopeo on:

```
	// When retrieving the digest from a registry >= 2.3 use the following header:
	//   "Accept": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json"
	headers := make(map[string][]string)
	headers["Accept"] = []string{manifest.DockerV2Schema2MediaType}
```

I presume the manifest is written by `buildah` when building?

Ah, thanks; this is an OCI image, and the code is incorrectly asking for schema2 images only. That should be easy to fix.

In the meantime, configuring buildah/podman to use the “docker” schema2 format instead of the OCI one may help — but of course only for newly pushed images.
Thanks for the response @mtrmac , would you like me to close this issue off? or will we close it once the issue is resolved?
No, please keep this open to track that the fix needs to be implemented.

Fix for c/image: https://github.com/containers/image/pull/646
Vendored into skopeo: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/680
Reopening as https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/680 must get in for Skopeo.
